# @andes/list

<br />

A collection of styled options.

<br />

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [List](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/992fd4-list/b/26f535)_.

<br />

## How to use

<br />

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

npm install --save @andes/list

```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```css

@import '@andes/list/index';

```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```css
/* Base */
@import '@andes/list/base';

/* Dropdown */
@import '@andes/list/lib/styles/dropdown';

/* Selectable */
@import '@andes/list/lib/styles/selectable';

/* List Groups */
@import '@andes/list/lib/styles/list-groups';

/* List Item disabled */
@import '@andes/list/lib/styles/list-item-disabled';

/* List Item selectable */
@import '@andes/list/lib/styles/list-item-selected';
```

> Always import `@andes/list/base` and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the components in your page:

```jsx

import { List, ListActionItem, ListGroup, ListItem } from '@andes/list';

```

## Public API

### `<List>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | `<ListItem>` / `<ListGroup>` | `node` | `null`  | |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `id` | List unique Id, used for a11y improvements. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `selectable` | Whether the items are selectable. | `boolean` | `true` |  |
| `srLabel` | <sup>1</sup>Label that describes the list's contents for screen readers (a11y) | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `type` | Defines the type of the list. One of `'default'` or `'dropdown'`. | `string` | `'default'` |  |
| `withDividers` | Add a divider between each item. | `boolean` | `false` |  |

> 1- `srLabel` should be provided for accessibility reasons.

<br />

### `<ListItem>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content. [See slot](#slots). | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `component` | <sup>2</sup>Html tag used for the root node. To avoid creating invalid HTML, only use it if you want to compose.  | `string: 'li', 'div'` | `'li'` |  |
| `description` | Text or component to display as description | `string` or `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `paddingSize` | Padding of the Item. It can be `0`, `16`, `24`, `32`, `40` | `string` | `16` |  |
| `leftContent` | <sup>1</sup>Node to be shown in the left side. | `node` | `null` |  |
| `rightContent` | <sup>2</sup>Text or component to display as subcontent information | `string`, `node` or `object` | `undefined` |  |
| `rightContentPosition` | <sup>3</sup>Position of rightContent. It can be `'top'` or `'centered'` | `string` | `'top'` |  |
| `selected` | Whether the item is selected. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `size` | Size of the list item. It can be `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'` | `string` | `'medium'` |  |
| `title` | Text to display as title | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `value` | Value of the list item (only available when it's used inside a ListBox component) | `string` | `undefined` |  |

> 1 - The current object format `{ title: string, src: string, alt: string, src2x: string }` will be deprecated from the `leftContent` options. Please use a `node` instead.

> 2 -  `rightContent` object:

```jsx
{
  content: string,
  description: string,  // Description will only be shown when content is a String and ListItem size !== 'small
}
```

> 3 -  If an image or Thumbnail is used as `leftContent`, the position will always be centered.

<br />

### `<ListActionItem>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content. Used only in very specific cases. Use `title`, `description` and `rightContent` to populate the item. | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `component` |<sup>1</sup> Html tag used for the root node. To avoid creating invalid HTML, only use it if you want to compose.  | `string: 'li', 'div'` | `'li'` |  |
| `description` | Text or component to display as description. | `string` or `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the item should be disabled. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `href` | Location where to point the item. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `id` | ListActionItem unique Id, used for a11y improvements. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `leftContent` | Node to be shown in the left side. | `node` | `null` |  |
| `maxLines` | The maximum quantity of lines to display. | `number` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClick` | Action to perform when the item is clicked. | `function` | `null` |  |
| `paddingSize` | Padding of the Item. It can be `0`, `16`, `24`, `32`, `40`. | `oneOf([0, 16, 24, 32, 40])` | `undefined` |  |
| `rightContent` | Text or component to display as subcontent information. | `string`, `node` or `object` | `undefined` |  |
| `rightContentPosition` | Position of rightContent. It can be `'top'` or `'centered'`. | `string` | `'top'` |  |
| `size` | Size of the list item. It can be `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'`. | `string` | `'medium'` |  |
| `target` | Where to display the provided href. Same as `<a>` attribute `target`. | `string: '_self', '_blank', '_parent', '_top'` | `undefined` |  |
| `title` | Text to display as title. | `string` | `undefined` |  |

> 1 -  `component` prop will be deprecated in a future major version. Please remove this prop to ensure future compatibility.

<br />

### `<ListGroup>`

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | `<ListItem>` | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `component` | Html tag used for the root node. To avoid creating invalid HTML, only use it if you want to compose.  | `string: 'ul', 'ol'` | `'ul'` |  |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `label` | Label to show over the group | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `paddingSize` | Padding of the group header. It can be `0`, `16`, `24`, `32`, `40` | `string` | `16` |  |
| `size` | Size of the group header. It can be `'small'`, `'medium'`, `'large'` | `string` | `'medium'` |  |
| `withDividers` | Add a divider between each item. | `boolean` | `false` |  |  |

<br />

### `<ListBox>`

ListBox is an `internal component`, if you need to use it please contact us to follow the use case.

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | `<ListItem>` / `<ListGroup>` | `node` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultSelectedKeys` | Selectable list's initial set of values (uncontrolled behavior). | `array` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Selectable list unique Id. Used for a11y improvements. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClick` | Function to execute when an option is clicked. | `function: (event, itemValue) => {}` | `undefined` |  |
| `onSelect` | Function to execute when an option is selected/unselected. | `function: (event, selectedValues, itemDetails) => {}` | `undefined` |  |
| `selectedKeys` | Selectable list's initial set of values (controlled behavior). | `array` | `undefined` |  |
| `selectionMode` | Allows single or multiple item selection. | `string: 'single', 'multiple'` | `single` |  |
| `shouldUseVirtualFocus` | Whether the listbox items should use virtual focus instead of being focused directly. | `bool` | `undefined` |  |
| `srLabel` | <sup>1</sup>ListBox label for screen readers. | `string` |  | ✅ |

> 1- `srLabel` should be provided for accesibility reasons.

<br />

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for List](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/list--andes-default-list).

### Base

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Base.png" alt="List Base Example" width="360" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div
    style={{ margin: '30px auto 0', width: '300px' }}
  >
    <List srLabel="Base List example" withDividers>
      <ListItem title="Title cell" description="Subtitle cell" />
      <ListItem title="Title cell" description="Subtitle cell" />
      <ListItem title="Title cell" description="Subtitle cell" />
    </List>
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

### Quick Selection

A Navigation List.

> [!NOTE]  
>
> - Providing the `href` prop to `ListItem`, it will render an anchor tag element with a11y included out of the box.
> - Proving the `onClick` prop to `ListItem` and without the `href` prop, it will render a button element with a11y included out of the box.
> - In case none of the above are provided to the `ListItem`, it will render just a text element.

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Quick-Selection.png" alt="Quick Selection List Example" width="360" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListActionItem } from '@andes/list';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div
    style={{ margin: '30px auto 0', width: '300px' }}
  >
    <List srLabel="Navigation List example" withDividers>
      <ListActionItem
        title="Title cell"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        href="#"
      />
      <ListActionItem
        title="Title cell"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        href="#"
      />
      <ListActionItem
        title="Title cell"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        href="#"
      />
    </List>
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

### Thumbnail & Icon

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Left-Content.png" alt="List Left Content Example" width="360" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import Share16 from '@andes/icons/Share16';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div
    style={{ margin: '30px auto 0', width: '300px' }}
  >
    <List srLabel="List with LeftContent example" withDividers>
      <ListItem
        title="Title cell default"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        leftContent={
          <Thumbnail size="40">
            <Share16 />
          </Thumbnail>
        }
      />
      <ListItem
        title="Title cell default"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        leftContent={
          <Thumbnail size="40">
            <Share16 />
          </Thumbnail>
        }
      />
      <ListItem
        title="Title cell default"
        description="Subtitle cell default"
        leftContent={
          <Thumbnail size="40">
            <Share16 />
          </Thumbnail>
        }
      />
    </List>
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

### Group

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Group.png" alt="List Group Example" width="360" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListGroup, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import './styles.scss';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div
    style={{ margin: '30px auto 0', width: '300px' }}
  >
    <List srLabel="Group List example">
      <ListGroup label="First group">
        <ListItem
          title="Title cell default"
          description="Subtitle cell default"
        />
        <ListItem
          title="Title cell default"
          description="Subtitle cell default"
        />
      </ListGroup>
      <ListGroup label="Second group">
        <ListItem
          title="Title cell default"
          description="Subtitle cell default"
        />
        <ListItem
          title="Title cell default"
          description="Subtitle cell default"
        />
        <ListItem
          title="Title cell default"
          description="Subtitle cell default"
        />
      </ListGroup>
    </List>
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

The list allows you to modify the following attributes depending on its reference value.

| Sub Attribute | Description           |
| ------------- | --------------------- |
| `border`      | List border color.    |
| `fill`        | List fill color.      |
| `icon`        | List icon color.      |
| `text`        | List text color.      |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    list: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          [status]: fillColorValue
        },
        title: {
          [status]: baseColor,
        },
        description: {
          [status]: baseColor,
        },
        icon: {
          [position]: iconColorValue
        },
        border: borderColor,
      },
      chevron: {
        color: {
          icon: {
            [status]: fillColorValue
          },
        },
      },
      pickList: {
        color: {
          selected: {
            title: {
              [status]: fillColorValue
            },
            icon: {
              [status]: fillColorValue
            },
            pipe: {
              [status]: fillColorValue
            },
          },
        },
      },
      group: {
        header: {
          color: {
            fill: fillColorValue,
            text: fillColorValue,
            border: fillColorValue,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

Possible `status` values:

```jsx

state: default | hover | active | disabled


```

Possible `position` values:

```jsx

position: left | right


```

> More states may be added in the future, so we recommend consulting the @andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json file for more detailed information

#### Example

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Reference-Theming-Default.png" alt="Reference Theming List Example" width="720" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import Camera20 from '@andes/icons/Camera20';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    list: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          default: '#ECF2FF',
        },
        text: {
          default: '#2a66fa',
          description: {
            default: '#487cfc',
          },
        },
        icon: {
          left: '#2a66fa',
          right: '#2a66fa',
        },
        border: '#C4D5FF',
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <List srLabel="Product List" withDividers>
      <ListItem
        leftContent={<Camera20 />}
        title="Row title"
        description="Row description"
        rightContent={<Camera20 />}
      />
      <ListItem
        leftContent={<Camera20 />}
        title="Row title"
        description="Row description"
        rightContent={<Camera20 />}
      />
    </List>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    list: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes switches regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming List Example" width="720" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    list: {
      padding: '0 16px',
      borderRadius: '8px',
      boxShadow: '0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <List srLabel="Product List" withDividers>
      <ListItem title="Row title" paddingSize={0} />
      <ListItem title="Row title" paddingSize={0} />
      <ListItem title="Row title" paddingSize={0} />
      <ListItem title="Row title" paddingSize={0} />
    </List>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                       | Description                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `data-andes-list`                | List container.                                                                  |
| `data-andes-list-group`          | List group container.                                                                  |
| `data-andes-list-group-header`  | List group header.                                                                     |
| `data-andes-list-group-sublist` | List group sublist.                                                                    |
| `data-andes-list-item`           | List item container.                                                                  |
| `data-andes-list-item-size`      | List sizes. Can be: `'small'`, `'medium'`, or `'large'`                                                       |
| `data-andes-list-chevron`        | List chevron.                                                                    |
| `data-andes-list-title`          | List title.                                                                      |
| `data-andes-list-description`    | List description.                                                                |
| `data-andes-list-left-content`   | List left content.                                                                    |
| `data-andes-list-right-content`  | List right content.                                                                    |
| `data-andes-state`               | List item state. Can be: `'disabled'` or `'selected'`.                                                               |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming List Example" width="720" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    list: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-list]': {
          borderRadius: '8px',
          overflow: 'hidden',
        },
        '[data-andes-list-item]': {
          backgroundColor: '#ECF2FF',
          borderColor: '#C4D5FF',
        },
        '[data-andes-list-item-size="small"]': {
          height: '48px',
        },
        '[data-andes-list-item-size="medium"]': {
          height: '56px',
        },
        '[data-andes-list-item-size="large"]': {
          height: '64px',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <List srLabel="Product List" withDividers>
      <ListItem title="Row title" size="small" />
      <ListItem title="Row title" size="medium" />
      <ListItem title="Row title" size="large" />
    </List>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

## Slots

This component supports the use of slots, providing flexible content areas that can be occupied by other components, thus facilitating the composition of more complex structures. Although React does not have a specific feature called "slots," similar behavior can be achieved using props.

<br />

In Andes, we will have default slots, referred to as children, and named slots that depend on each specific component. This means we can define areas where custom content can be inserted, enhancing the flexibility and reusability of our components.

<br />

### Use of slots

The ListItem allows you to modify the following slots.

| Slot name            | Description                                               |
| -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `children (default)` | Allows for custom content in list rows. This slot is ideal for situations where the default list design does not meet the specific needs of the interface. |

```jsx
<List srLabel="Product List">
  <ListItem>
    Content of the Row Slot
  </ListItem>
</List>
```

#### Example

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Example-Reference-Slot.png" alt="Example of Slots in a List" width="575" />

```jsx
/* CustomLayout.js */
import React from 'react';
import PlaceholderImage16 from '@andes/icons/PlaceholderImage16';
import OverflowMenuVertical24 from '@andes/icons/OverflowMenuVertical24';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import { ThumbnailMultiple, Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { Text } from '@andes/typography';
import './customLayout.scss';

const CustomLayout = () => (
  <div className="layout">
    <div className="layout__first-column">
      <ThumbnailMultiple
        modifier="square"
        containerSize="72"
        type="grid"
        overflow={6}
      >
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
      </ThumbnailMultiple>
    </div>

    <div className="layout__second-column">
      <div className="custom-content">
        <Text size="l" weight="semibold">Gaming</Text>
        <div className="custom-content__text-icon">
          <PlaceholderImage16 />
          <Text size="xs">Lista de deseos</Text>
        </div>
        <Text size="xs" color="secondary">Privada</Text>
      </div>
      <Text size="xs" color="secondary">3 productos</Text>
    </div>

    <div className="layout__third-column">
      <FloatingMenu
        trigger={(
          <button className="custom-button" aria-label="Actions">
            <OverflowMenuVertical24 />
          </button>
        )}
      >
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 1" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 2" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 3" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 4" />
      </FloatingMenu>
    </div>
  </div>
);

export default CustomLayout;
```

```css
/* customLayout.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
@import '@andes/typography/index';
@import '@andes/checkbox/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';

.layout {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto;
  grid-gap: 8px;
  justify-content: start;
  padding-top: $andes-spacing-20;
  padding-bottom: $andes-spacing-20;
}

.layout__second-column {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: $andes-spacing-8;
}

.custom-content {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: $andes-spacing-4;
}

.custom-content__text-icon {
  display: flex;
  gap: $andes-spacing-4;
}

.custom-button {
  @include add-focus-visible();

  display: block;
  width: $andes-spacing-24;
  height: $andes-spacing-24;
  background-color: transparent;
  padding: 0;
  margin: 0;
  border: 0;
}
```

```jsx
/* App.js */
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListItem } from '@andes/list';
import CustomLayout from './CustomLayout';
import './styles.scss';

const App = () => {
  return (
    <List srLabel="Product List" selectable={false}>
      <ListItem>
        <CustomLayout />
      </ListItem>
    </List>
  )
}
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```

<img src="resources/list/docs/List-Actionable-Example-Reference-Slot.png" alt="Example of Slots in a List Actionable" width="575" />

```jsx
/* CustomLayout.js */
import React from 'react';
import PlaceholderImage16 from '@andes/icons/PlaceholderImage16';
import OverflowMenuVertical24 from '@andes/icons/OverflowMenuVertical24';
import { FloatingMenu, FloatingMenuItem } from '@andes/floating-menu';
import { ThumbnailMultiple, Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { Text } from '@andes/typography';
import './customLayout.scss';

const CustomLayout = () => (
  <div className="layout">
    <div className="layout__first-column">
      <ThumbnailMultiple
        modifier="square"
        containerSize="72"
        type="grid"
        overflow={6}
      >
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
        <Thumbnail>
          <img
            alt="avatar"
            src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/D_Q_NP_221705-MLA25074486775_092016-I.webp"
          />
        </Thumbnail>
      </ThumbnailMultiple>
    </div>

    <div className="layout__second-column">
      <div className="custom-content">
        <Text size="l" weight="semibold">Gaming</Text>
        <div className="custom-content__text-icon">
          <PlaceholderImage16 />
          <Text size="xs">Lista de deseos</Text>
        </div>
        <Text size="xs" color="secondary">Privada</Text>
      </div>
      <Text size="xs" color="secondary">3 productos</Text>
    </div>

    <div className="layout__third-column">
      <FloatingMenu
        trigger={(
          <button className="custom-button" aria-label="Actions">
            <OverflowMenuVertical24 />
          </button>
        )}
      >
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 1" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 2" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 3" />
        <FloatingMenuItem title="Action 4" />
      </FloatingMenu>
    </div>
  </div>
);

export default CustomLayout;
```

```css
/* customLayout.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
@import '@andes/typography/index';
@import '@andes/checkbox/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/index';

.layout {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto;
  grid-gap: 8px;
  justify-content: start;
  padding-top: $andes-spacing-20;
  padding-bottom: $andes-spacing-20;
}

.layout__second-column {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: $andes-spacing-8;
}

.custom-content {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: $andes-spacing-4;
}

.custom-content__text-icon {
  display: flex;
  gap: $andes-spacing-4;
}
```

```jsx
/* App.js */
import React from 'react';
import { List, ListActionItem } from '@andes/list';
import CustomLayout from './CustomLayout';
import './styles.scss';

const App = () => {
  return (
    <List srLabel="Product List">
      <ListActionItem onClick={() => {}}>
        <CustomLayout />
      </ListActionItem>
    </List>
  )
}
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/list/index';
```
